title: pimpinella saxifraga var. procera
intro: Pimpinella saxifraga var. procera, also known as burnet saxifrage or burnet, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of burnet include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and relieving symptoms of colds and flu. Burnet is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be consumed as a fresh or dried herb in salads and soups.
